text: A Roman mosaic artist uses Roman numerals to label his designs. He calculates the area of a rectangular mosaic by multiplying its width (XXIV) by its length (XXXVII). What is the area of the mosaic?
generatorSolution:
Step 1: Convert the Roman numerals to standard numbers.
XXIV = 10 + 10 + 4 = 24
XXXVII = 10 + 10 + 10 + 5 + 1 + 1 = 37

Step 2: Multiply 24 × 37.
Break this into parts: 24 × 37 = 24 × 30 + 24 × 7
24 × 30 = 720
24 × 7 = 168

Step 3: Add the parts together.
720 + 168 = 888

The area of the mosaic is 888.
